coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 6–8 miles, Z2 (130–140 bpm), rolling terrain. You're in week 23 of a 22-week plan—you're tapered and recovered hard (RHR 57, 9.5h sleep). Tuesday is normally quality day, but in taper, quality means *maintaining* leg turnover without metabolic stress. This isn't a threshold or VO2 workout. Keep HR under 140, focus on smooth footfalls on uneven ground to prep for alpine technicality, and call it done by 8 miles max. You've earned restraint.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Front-load carbs today: 250–300g distributed across breakfast, lunch, pre-run, and post-run. Your glycogen stores are critical in taper—you're not burning it off as aggressively as peak phase, so carbs won't spike excess weight. Aim for 60–80g carbs within 30 min post-run with 15–20g protein. Given your travel schedule, prioritize simple carbs (rice, pasta, fruit, bagels) over fiber-heavy options that could complicate digestion before race week.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: You've had exceptional sleep the last two nights (9.5h at 93% efficiency), and your recovery score is maxed. Protect it: 9+ hours again tonight, cool room (≤65°F), no screens after 21:30. Your resting HR at 57 is excellent—stay there. In 80 days, you need the nervous system calm and parasympathetic tone high. This sleep volume is your competitive edge right now; don't trade it for work email.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 80 days out and fully recovered—resist the urge to "make up" for taper volume by adding mileage or intensity. The impulse is normal; it's also how athletes derail peaking phases. Your recent runs show you're fit (12mi at Z2 HR 131 is solid), and taper works by *rest*, not by panic. Use these next three weeks to nail logistics, study the Julian Alps course profile, and mentally rehearse the 2,500m vert. That's where your competitive advantage lives.
